examples.title: Algorithm: IoT Device Data Collection & Transmission
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the core operation flow of an IoT endpoint device - sensing, processing, and communication
examples.code:
ALGORITHM IoT_Device_Operation
INPUT: Sensor configuration, Communication protocol settings
OUTPUT: Transmitted sensor data to cloud/gateway

CONSTANTS:
 MAX_RETRY = 3
 SAMPLE_INTERVAL = 60 // seconds

BEGIN
 INITIALIZE()
 // Initialize all hardware components
 Initialize sensors (temperature, humidity, motion)
 Initialize actuators (LED, relay)
 Initialize communication interface (WiFi/Bluetooth)
 Connect to network/gateway
 
 WHILE device_is_active DO
 // Step 1: Sense - Collect data from environment
 FOR each sensor IN connected_sensors DO
 raw_value = sensor.read()
 calibrated_value = calibrate(raw_value, sensor.type)
 sensor_data[sensor.name] = calibrated_value
 END FOR
 
 // Step 2: Process - Analyze and prepare data
 processed_data = package_data(sensor_data)
 timestamp = get_current_time()
 payload = create_json_payload(timestamp, processed_data)
 
 // Step 3: Actuate - Perform actions based on thresholds
 IF temperature > THRESHOLD_HIGH THEN
 activate_cooling()
 END IF
 
 // Step 4: Communicate - Transmit data
 success = transmit_to_gateway(payload)
 
 IF NOT success THEN
 retry_count = 0
 WHILE retry_count < MAX_RETRY AND NOT success DO
 wait(5) // Wait before retry
 success = transmit_to_gateway(payload)
 retry_count = retry_count + 1
 END WHILE
 IF NOT success THEN
 store_locally(payload) // Store for later transmission
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // Step 5: Sleep for power efficiency
 enter_low_power_mode(SAMPLE_INTERVAL)
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates the five key stages of IoT device operation: 1) Sensing - reading environmental data from sensors, 2) Processing - calibrating and packaging the data, 3) Actuating - responding to thresholds by controlling actuators, 4) Communicating - transmitting data to a gateway or cloud, and 5) Power management - entering sleep mode to conserve energy. This cycle represents the fundamental operation of any IoT endpoint device.

examples.title: Python: Simulated IoT Sensor Data Collection
examples.description: Python implementation demonstrating sensor data collection, processing, and JSON payload creation for IoT endpoints
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates key IoT device concepts: 1) Sensor classes with calibration logic representing different sensor types (temperature, humidity, motion), 2) Data collection from multiple sensors, 3) JSON payload creation for transmission, and 4) Simulation of the device operation cycle. The code shows how real IoT devices collect environmental data, process it, and prepare it for communication - exactly what physical endpoints do in the real world.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of sensors per reading cycle

examples.title: C (Arduino): Temperature Monitor with Actuator Control
examples.description: C code for Arduino demonstrating sensor reading, threshold-based actuator control, and serial communication
examples.explanation: This Arduino C code demonstrates a complete IoT endpoint device: 1) Temperature sensor reading with multi-sample averaging for accuracy, 2) Threshold-based actuator control (fan, LED, buzzer), 3) Serial communication for data transmission simulation, and 4) Sleep mode for power efficiency. This represents exactly how real IoT physical devices work - sensing the environment, processing the data, acting on it, and communicating it. The code includes proper comments explaining each hardware interaction.
